forecast.content:
今（20）日環境風場為偏東風，清晨至上午西半部擴散條件差，午後已逐漸轉好，污染物稍易累積；須留意春節期間，各地節慶活動可能造成短時間污染物濃度上升。依18時監測結果，花東空品區為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、中部、雲嘉南、高屏、宜蘭空品區及金門、澎湖為「普通」等級；馬祖為「橘色提醒」等級。
依監測資料及氣象署20日18時氣象資料顯示：21日環境風場為偏東風至東南風，西半部污染物易累積，午後臭氧濃度稍易上升；須留意春節期間，各地節慶活動可能造成短時間污染物濃度上升；清晨至上午中部以北地區及晚間馬祖、金門易有低雲或局部霧影響能見度。宜蘭、花東空品區為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、中部空品區及馬祖、澎湖為「普通」等級；雲嘉南、高屏空品區及金門為「橘色提醒」等級。
22日環境風場為東南風至偏東風，西半部污染物易累積，午後臭氧濃度稍易上升；須留意春節期間，各地節慶活動可能造成短時間污染物濃度上升；馬祖、金門易有低雲或局部霧影響能見度。花東空品區為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、宜蘭空品區及馬祖、澎湖為「普通」等級；中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及金門為「橘色提醒」等級。
23日環境風場為偏東風至東南風，西半部污染物易累積，午後臭氧濃度稍易上升。花東空品區為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、宜蘭空品區及馬祖、金門、澎湖為「普通」等級；中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及為「橘色提醒」等級。
未來一週，21日至24日環境風場為偏東風至東南風，西半部位於背風側，污染物易累積；25日鋒面通過及東北季風稍增強，環境風場轉為東北風，東北風可能挾帶微量境外污染物移入，中南部污染物仍稍易累積；26日東北季風減弱，環境風場為東北風至偏東風，西半部位於背風側，污染物易累積。空氣品質受氣象條件影響大，有其不確定性，請隨時留意最新空氣品質資訊。
forecast.summary_en: Northern Taiwan faces persistent easterly winds through at least February 26th, with the western half in a pollution-trapping pattern — ozone concentrations will climb in afternoons, and particulate matter will accumulate, especially during Lunar New Year festivities. Today through the 23rd, most northern regions hold "moderate" air quality, but central and southern zones are already at "orange alert" levels. A frontal system on the 25th will briefly strengthen northeasterly winds and may draw in trace transboundary pollution before conditions deteriorate again.

今（22）日環境風場為東南風至偏東風，清晨至上午西半部擴散條件較差，污染物易累積。依10時監測結果，花東空品區為「良好」等級；竹苗、高屏、宜蘭空品區及澎湖為「普通」等級；北部、中部空品區及馬祖、金門地區為「橘色提醒」等級；雲嘉南空品區為「紅色警示」等級。
依監測資料及氣象署22日10時氣象資料顯示：22日中國沿海污染物濃度偏高，臺灣受海峽污染物影響，且環境風場為東南風至偏東風，清晨至上午西半部垂直及水平擴散條件較差，污染物濃度易上升；午後受光化作用影響，須留意臭氧濃度上升；馬祖及西半部清晨易有低雲或局部霧影響能見度。花東為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、高屏、宜蘭空品區及澎湖為「普通」等級，竹苗以北及高屏地區短時間達橘色提醒等級；中部、雲嘉南空品區及馬祖、金門為「橘色提醒」等級，彰化至雲林一帶清晨至上午因海峽污染物濃度偏高且水平及垂直擴散條件不佳，空氣品質短時間達紅色警示等級。

2026-02-24.narration_text:
Good evening, and welcome to your late-night check-in for Tuesday, February 24th, heading into Wednesday the 25th.

Nothing urgent to flag tonight — the heads-up list is clear. Right now it's a thick, soupy evening: twenty-three degrees but it feels clammy and close, with humidity sitting at ninety-six percent — that's genuinely soggy air, the kind that makes your skin feel damp just standing still. The wind is completely calm, overcast skies overhead, and the ground is dry despite how heavy the air feels. AQI is seventy-three, moderate — nothing alarming but not sparkling either.

Now for the next twenty-four hours. Tomorrow is honestly a pretty pleasant and uneventful day — a welcome exhale after tonight's sticky air. Overnight through about six in the morning, we're sitting around twenty degrees with humidity still high at ninety-four percent and a slight chance of rain — unlikely but worth noting if you hear anything on the roof before dawn. As you wake up and head into the morning, the air dries out noticeably to around eighty-five percent humidity, temperatures ease up to about twenty-one degrees, and a breezy wind picks up to keep things feeling fresh. Mixed clouds through the day, rain staying very unlikely all the way through the afternoon. The evening brings a gentle breeze and temperatures dipping back toward nineteen degrees — still comfortable, still dry. AQI is forecast to stay in the moderate range, with a front passing through that should gradually shift winds to the northeast and help clear things out. Bottom line — the morning and afternoon are yours to enjoy, rain gear can stay home, and it's a solid day for getting Dad outside.

On forecast accuracy: yesterday I called for a pleasant morning and said to carry an umbrella for the evening commute, with the night being warm enough to skip climate control. The actual conditions today came in at twenty-three degrees with ninety-five percent humidity — warmer and much stickier than the forecast implied, and the evening never really dried out. The overall temperature call was close, but I underestimated how heavy the humidity would be throughout the day. We'll call that one close but not quite.

2026-02-27.narration_text:
Good evening, everyone. Nothing urgent to flag tonight — no major alerts to kick things off, so let's ease into it.

Right now at just before eleven, it feels like a damp, mild eighteen point seven degrees outside with humidity sitting at eighty-six percent — that's the kind of air that clings to you, heavy and a little muggy. There's a light breeze drifting in from the east-northeast, barely enough to stir the leaves. The sky is fully overcast and the ground is wet — we've had eighteen and a half millimeters of rain today, so everything out there is soaked. AQI is thirty-seven, which is good. One health note before we move on: with humidity this high and the air this thick, anyone in the family managing Meniere's disease should take it slow tonight. High humidity like this can be enough to tip the balance toward dizziness or vertigo. Stay hydrated, avoid any sudden head movements, and keep your medication somewhere easy to reach. Take it easy.

Now for the next twenty-four hours. Tomorrow is honestly a tale of two halves, and the swing is a big one. We start the morning around fifteen point nine degrees — cool, breezy, mixed clouds, very unlikely to rain. That's actually a fresh and pleasant early start. But through late morning the temperature climbs hard, and by midday it feels like twenty-six degrees with the sky going fully overcast and humidity pushing to ninety-one percent — sticky and close. That afternoon warmth doesn't last into the evening; overnight things settle back to around eighteen and a half degrees, still overcast, with rain chances nudging up to forty percent. If you're a light sleeper, you may hear some rain on the roof before dawn Sunday. Bottom line — enjoy the cool morning, layer up for the temperature swing by noon, and don't be surprised if Sunday starts wet.

As for yesterday's forecast — I called a tale of two halves with a big temperature swing and said we'd start around fifteen point nine degrees cool and breezy. What actually happened was a much more muted day: the apparent temperature came in at eighteen point nine degrees with eighty-six percent humidity and virtually no wind. The dramatic climb I predicted didn't materialize — conditions stayed fairly flat and mild all day. That one missed the mark on the temperature arc, though the overcast and humid character of the day was about right.

2026-02-28.narration_text:
Smooth sailing tonight — no active weather alerts for the Shulin and Banqiao area. Right now it feels like 21 degrees, but the air is near-saturated at 90 percent humidity, so everything feels damp and close against your skin. Wind is barely a whisper out of the SSE, the sky is fully overcast, and the ground is dry. Air quality is good with an AQI of 33 — slightly better than yesterday's already clean reading. Anyone sensitive to high humidity — particularly if Ménière's is a concern — take it easy this evening, stay well hydrated, and avoid sudden movements that could trigger dizziness. Light layers are all you need tonight; no rain gear required before bed.

Yesterday's beds were asked to dry out, and one more dry night helps — hold off watering again tomorrow morning and check that saucers are empty. The morning commute toward Sanxia and Shulin should be comfortable at around 21 degrees with overcast skies and rain unlikely, but humidity will be thick so keep windows cracked only slightly. The evening return warms to around 24 degrees with a light breeze — still overcast but no hazards flagged either way.

Mid-morning is the window for Dad. Head to the Shulin Riverside Greenway — the flat paved five-kilometre loop with shaded sections and water stations is ideal for steady footing. Get out by nine and turn back before eleven, well ahead of the afternoon heat surge. The surface is paved and Parkinson's-friendly, so no cane needed on this route.

Tonight, a bowl of ma you ji — sesame oil chicken — is exactly right for this sticky, damp close-of-day. For climate control, run the AC in dry mode and keep windows closed; the outdoor dew point will be high through tomorrow afternoon, and letting that moisture in will make the house feel swampy.

Tomorrow runs hot and humid before easing. The morning starts overcast near 21 degrees, then heat surges fast to a muggy 31 degrees around lunch — that is your biggest watch point. By evening it pulls back to 26, then overnight a breezy front nudges temperatures down toward 21. The bigger shift arrives Tuesday evening into Wednesday, when a front drops temperatures sharply and rain becomes likely. Enjoy the warmth while it lasts — Wednesday will feel like a different season.

Today's forecast was close. The overnight temperature and humidity landed almost exactly on target, though the predicted afternoon peak was bold — we will see how Sunday's surge verifies tomorrow.

2026-03-01.narration_text:
Smooth sailing on the alert front tonight. Right now it feels like 24.9 degrees outside, near-saturated and clammy — the kind of air that sticks to your skin the moment you step out. A light air from the west is barely moving, ground is wet from earlier rain, and AQI sits at 61, moderate. Anyone sensitive to PM2.5 should keep that in mind. The high humidity is a real concern for Ménière's — if anyone in the family deals with that, take it slow tonight and keep the inner ear warm and dry. Grab a light layer and carry an umbrella; rain gear is needed.

Soil is well watered after today's rain, so hold off on any watering tomorrow and check that saucers and beds aren't pooling. For the commute toward Sanxia and Shulin, the morning leg feels around 18 degrees with a light breeze and rain unlikely — mixed clouds, roads should be manageable. By the evening run home it will be warmer and overcast around 23 degrees, still unlikely to rain but clouds will have thickened through the afternoon, so leave a little buffer.

Outdoor conditions are rated avoid all day tomorrow, so keep Dad inside. Mid-morning, head over to the Banqiao Far Eastern Department Store Sky Garden — elevator access, indoor tile and a sheltered terrace, flat and safe underfoot for Parkinson's. It is a comfortable loop with plenty of seating if he needs to rest.

A bowl of niu rou mian is exactly right for this sticky, cool-and-damp mood tonight — warm and grounding. Run the AC in dry mode to pull the moisture out of the air; the dew point is high enough that dehumidifying matters more than cooling right now. Keep windows closed given the moderate AQI.

Tomorrow runs warm then tumbles. Morning starts overcast and near-saturated around 20 degrees, then clouds break and it surges to nearly 29 degrees around lunch. By evening clouds close back in and rain becomes likely as a front moves through, dragging temperatures down toward 16. Wednesday stays rainy and cool. Get any outdoor business done before mid-afternoon.

Today's forecast called a front arriving with rain by evening and temperatures dropping sharply — that front is confirmed, though the current apparent temperature of 24.9 degrees ran warmer than the predicted 16-degree evening low, suggesting the timing slipped a few hours later than forecast.

2026-03-02.narration_text:
Smooth sailing — no weather alerts this morning. Right now it feels like 23 degrees with near-saturated air, so expect that damp, close feeling on your skin the moment you step outside. A light breeze is coming in from the west-northwest, and the ground is wet from overnight drizzle. AQI sits at 67, which is moderate — sensitive family members should limit prolonged outdoor exposure. The high humidity today may aggravate Ménière's symptoms, so take it slow, avoid sudden head movements, and keep noise low if dizziness is a concern. Grab a light jacket and carry an umbrella just in case.

Soil is still saturated from yesterday's rain, so hold off on watering and check that drainage channels are clear before the next round arrives tonight. The morning commute toward Shulin and Sanxia should be fine — near 20 degrees, overcast, with precipitation unlikely, though roads are still damp so leave extra stopping distance. The evening return warms to around 23 degrees with overcast skies and a light breeze — also manageable, but clouds will thicken again by then, so keep that umbrella handy.

Mid-morning is the best window for Dad today. Head to the Banqiao Lin Family Garden — flat paved garden paths, large banyan shade, and plenty of seating make it one of the most Parkinson's-friendly spots in the area. A gentle stroll before the afternoon heat builds is ideal; avoid the Shulin Riverside Greenway today as wet paved surfaces near the river can be slippery.

Tonight calls for a bowl of si shen tang — the herbal pork rib soup is a perfect match for this cool, damp evening and supports circulation on a sticky day. Run the AC in dry mode and the dehumidifier together this afternoon for about five to six hours to pull the moisture out of the air indoors. AQI is moderate, so keep windows closed while dehumidifying.

Today runs warm then tumbles sharply. The morning stays overcast and sticky near 20 degrees, then clouds break and temperatures surge to nearly 29 degrees around lunch — that is the day's peak. By this evening clouds close back in and rain becomes likely as a front pushes through, dragging temperatures down toward 16 overnight and into tomorrow. Wednesday stays rainy and cool with rain likely through the morning. Get outdoor business done before mid-afternoon today.

Yesterday's forecast called for a warm surge to near 29 degrees followed by a front and rain — today's data confirms the warm surge and the front arriving this evening, so the forecast was spot on.

今（3）日受東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，南部位於下風處，稍易累積污染物，午後隨著華南雲雨區東移，全臺降雨機率上升，空氣品質可望降為良好等級。依10時監測結果，竹苗、宜蘭、花東空品區及馬祖、金門、澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區為「普通」等級。
依監測資料及氣象署3月3日10時氣象資料顯示：3日東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，南部位於下風處，稍易累積污染物，午後隨著華南雲雨區東移，全臺降雨機率上升，空氣品質多為良好等級。竹苗、中部、雲嘉南、宜蘭、花東空品區及馬祖、金門、澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、高屏空品區為「普通」等級。
4日東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場仍為東北風，中南部位於下風處，污染物稍易累積。竹苗、宜蘭、花東空品區及澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及馬祖、金門為「普通」等級。
5日環境風場轉為偏東風，西半部位於背風側，擴散條件稍差，污染物稍易累積。宜蘭、花東空品區及澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及馬祖、金門為「普通」等級。
未來一週，3日至4日東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，中南部污染物稍易累積；5日環境風場轉為偏東風，西半部污染物稍易累積；6日至8日東北季風再增強影響，環境風場為東北風，東北風可能挾帶微量境外污染物移入，影響情況視上游濃度累積及降雨情況而有所變化，中南部污染物稍易累積。空氣品質受氣象條件影響大，有其不確定性，請隨時留意最新空氣品質資訊。

2026-03-03.narration_text:
今天梅尼爾氏症需要留意。濕度近飽和，露點與氣溫幾乎貼齊，整個人會感覺黏悶不舒服，這正是梅尼爾氏症容易發作的條件，請減少快速起身或轉頭的動作。體感溫度只有十五度出頭，比昨天的二十三度涼了許多，空氣品質良好，AQI三十一。地面因今天累積降雨已濕滑，出門請穿防滑鞋，帶雨具，上身加一件薄外套。

花園昨天已確認排水，今天持續觀察盆底積水即可，不需澆水，土壤已充分濕潤。早上通勤路段三峽與樹林一帶體感偏涼，降雨機率不高，但路面仍濕，開車或騎車都要放慢速度；傍晚回程雲層依然厚，備好外套。

今天戶外指數評等為避免外出，地面濕滑且雲層全覆。建議爸爸上午前往板橋遠東百貨空中花園，電梯直達、室內磁磚平整、有遮蔽露臺可休息，帕金森氏症友善。因為梅尼爾氏症中度警示，請全程坐著休息為主，避免快速移動。

天氣涼濕，今晚適合喝一碗麻油雞暖暖身子。空調今天建議關閉，室外AQI良好可短暫開窗換氣，但濕度近飽和，窗戶不宜開太久，以免室內更悶。

明天清晨仍陰涼，體感約十一度，微風，近飽和濕度，出門前請多加一層。關鍵轉折在午餐前後，雲層會逐漸散開，體感升至十八度，降雨機率低，是明天最舒適的時段。傍晚雲層再度變厚，氣溫回落。週六體感可衝到二十七度，享受難得的暖陽，但週日入夜後另一道鋒面靠近，雨具請繼續放在包包裡。

今天預報準確，體感十五度與預報吻合，梅尼爾氏症警示也如期觸發，唯一差異是降雨量達十二點五毫米，比預報描述的「可能降雨」稍多。

今（3）日受東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，南部位於下風處，稍易累積污染物，午後隨著華南雲雨區東移，全臺降雨機率上升，空氣品質可望降為良好等級。依18時監測結果，北部、竹苗、中部、宜蘭、花東空品區及澎湖多為「良好」等級；雲嘉南、高屏空品區及馬祖、金門地區為「普通」等級。
依監測資料及氣象署3月3日18時氣象資料顯示：4日東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，中南部位於下風處，稍易累積污染物。竹苗、宜蘭、花東空品區及澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及馬祖、金門為「普通」等級。
5日環境風場轉為偏東風，西半部位於背風側，擴散條件稍差，稍易累積污染物。宜蘭、花東空品區及澎湖為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、中部、雲嘉南、高屏空品區及馬祖、金門為「普通」等級。
6日東北季風再增強，環境風場為東北風，東北風可能挾帶微量境外污染物移入影響臺灣及離島地區，影響程度需視上游污染物累積及降雨情況有所改變，中南部位於下風處，稍有利污染物累積。花東空品區為「良好」等級；北部、竹苗、中部、雲嘉南、高屏、宜蘭空品區及馬祖、金門、澎湖為「普通」等級。
未來一週，4日東北季風或大陸冷氣團影響，環境風場為東北風，中南部污染物稍易累積；5日環境風場轉為偏東風，西半部污染物稍易累積；6日至9日東北季風再增強影響，環境風場為東北風，東北風可能挾帶微量境外污染物移入，影響情況視上游濃度累積及降雨情況而有所變化，中南部污染物稍易累積。空氣品質受氣象條件影響大，有其不確定性，請隨時留意最新空氣品質資訊。
aqi_forecast.summary_en: Tomorrow's air quality is Expected to be Good.
aqi_forecast.summary_zh: 明日北部空氣品質預測為「良好」等級。

2026-03-04.narration_text:
Smooth sailing this morning — no alerts to flag. Right now it feels like 13 degrees with a gentle easterly breeze, clammy but not dripping, and the ground is dry. Air quality is good with an AQI of 37, so no concerns there. Layer up before you head out — a light jacket is enough, and no rain gear needed today.

Soil is still holding moisture from the recent wet stretch, so hold off on watering and focus on checking pot drainage instead. The morning commute into Shulin or Sanxia looks calm — a light breeze, all clear on precip, and mixed clouds by the time you roll in. The evening ride home stays dry and comfortable, a touch warmer than the morning, so no surprises either way.

Mid-morning is the sweet spot for Dad today. Head to the Dahan River Bikeway at the Yingge section — flat paved trail, well-shaded, and easy to park. A gentle walk along the riverside is ideal; the surface is smooth and Parkinson's-friendly, with no steps to navigate.

Tonight calls for a steaming bowl of niu rou mian — perfectly matched to a cool and clammy evening around 18 degrees. Climate control can stay off today; the data shows no dehumidifier or AC needed, and with good air quality you can crack a window this afternoon to let the warmer air circulate.

Today's big story is the temperature climb. Clouds thin through the morning and the feel rises nearly 7 degrees by lunchtime — that afternoon stretch around 18 degrees is the day's most comfortable window. Overnight, clouds close back in and the feel slips to around 17, but rain stays unlikely. Tomorrow jumps sharply to 27 degrees before another front nudges in Friday evening and drags temperatures back down. Enjoy Thursday while it lasts.

Yesterday's forecast called for a comfortable 18 degrees by lunch and a mild overnight — today's actual morning reading of 13 degrees came in cooler than predicted, so the forecast ran a little warm on the overnight low.
